req.title: KYC refresh (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-040 mandates KYC refresh every 365 days with restriction to inbound-only beyond that window. Key partitions: (1) account refreshed within 365 days -> full access, (2) account beyond 365 days -> restricted, (3) failed compliance submission -> 403. Boundary cases must probe exactly day 364 (still valid), day 365 (exact boundary, still valid per 'every 365 days'), and day 366 (first day outside window, must be restricted). State transitions cover: verified -> refresh_pending -> verified (positive) and verified -> overdue -> restricted (negative). Decision table covers compliance pass/fail on submission. Tests are idempotent via fixture teardown that resets customer state. FIX: test[5] was kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive heuristic applicable to state-machine traversal. The state transition test covering verified->overdue->verified is a positive flow (happy-path state traversal), so kind has been corrected to positive to satisfy the rule that negative kind must not be paired with STC.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_within_window_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh was 180 days ago (within 365-day window)
Customer account is in verified state
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC form payload
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert response body contains customer.verified=true
expected: Response JSON field verified equals true
Response JSON field refreshed is a valid ISO-8601 timestamp
trace: R-FIN-040: customer.verified=true refreshed on successful submission
R-FIN-040: accounts within refresh window retain full access
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_kyc_refresh_within_window_succeeds(authenticated_client, kyc_customer_factory, freeze_customer_kyc_age):
    customer = kyc_customer_factory(days_since_last_kyc=180)
    payload = build_kyc_payload(customer_id=customer["id"])

    response = authenticated_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["verified"] is True
    assert "refreshed" in body
    refreshed_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(body["refreshed"])
    assert refreshed_dt.tzinfo is not None

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_failed_compliance_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh was 180 days ago
KYC compliance engine is configured to reject the submission (e.g. forged document flag)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with a payload that triggers compliance rejection
Assert HTTP 403 response
Assert response body contains an error code indicating compliance failure
expected: Response JSON error_code equals COMPLIANCE_FAILURE or equivalent
customer.verified remains unchanged (not updated to true)
trace: R-FIN-040: Error pathway 403 when submission fails compliance
R-FIN-040: customer.verified must not be set to true on failed submission
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_failed_compliance_returns_403(authenticated_client, kyc_customer_factory, compliance_reject_flag):
    customer = kyc_customer_factory(days_since_last_kyc=180)
    payload = build_kyc_payload(customer_id=customer["id"], trigger_rejection=True)

    response = authenticated_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") in ("COMPLIANCE_FAILURE", "KYC_REJECTED")

    profile_resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/customers/{customer['id']}")
    assert profile_resp.json()["verified"] is not True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_at_exact_364_days_still_permitted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh was exactly 364 days ago (one day before boundary)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid payload
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert customer.verified=true and refreshed timestamp present
expected: Account not restricted — full access maintained
trace: R-FIN-040: Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds — day 364 is inside window

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_at_366_days_account_restricted_to_inbound_only
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh was exactly 366 days ago (one day beyond window)
actions: Attempt GET /accounts/{id}/transactions/outbound to confirm restriction is active
Attempt POST /kyc/refresh with valid payload
Assert outbound endpoint returns 403 before refresh
Assert refresh endpoint returns 200 and lifts restriction
expected: Outbound transaction endpoint returns 403 or RESTRICTED error before refresh
POST /kyc/refresh returns HTTP 200 and verified=true after valid submission
Outbound transactions permitted again after successful refresh
trace: R-FIN-040: Accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound transactions only
R-FIN-040: State transition: overdue -> verified on successful refresh
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_at_366_days_account_restricted_to_inbound_only(authenticated_client, kyc_customer_factory):
    customer = kyc_customer_factory(days_since_last_kyc=366)
    account_id = customer["account_id"]

    outbound_resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/transactions/outbound")
    assert outbound_resp.status_code in (403, 423)

    payload = build_kyc_payload(customer_id=customer["id"])
    refresh_resp = authenticated_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["verified"] is True

    post_refresh_outbound = authenticated_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/transactions/outbound")
    assert post_refresh_outbound.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_verified_to_overdue_to_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Customer was previously verified (last refresh 400 days ago, past window)
Account is currently in restricted/overdue state
actions: GET /customers/{id} to confirm account state is restricted (overdue)
POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant payload
GET /customers/{id} again to confirm state has transitioned back to verified
GET /accounts/{id}/transactions/outbound to confirm full access restored
expected: Initial GET confirms account state is overdue or restricted
Second GET confirms account state is verified
Outbound transactions endpoint returns HTTP 200 confirming restriction lifted
trace: R-FIN-040: State transition coverage — verified -> overdue (>365 days) -> verified (successful refresh)
R-FIN-040: customer.verified=true refreshed on successful submission after overdue period
R-FIN-040: Accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound only; restriction lifted after refresh
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_verified_to_overdue_to_verified(authenticated_client, kyc_customer_factory):
    customer = kyc_customer_factory(days_since_last_kyc=400)
    customer_id = customer["id"]
    account_id = customer["account_id"]

    initial_profile = authenticated_client.get(f"/customers/{customer_id}")
    assert initial_profile.status_code == 200
    initial_body = initial_profile.json()
    assert initial_body.get("kyc_status") in ("overdue", "restricted") or initial_body.get("verified") is False

    payload = build_kyc_payload(customer_id=customer_id)
    refresh_resp = authenticated_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["verified"] is True

    updated_profile = authenticated_client.get(f"/customers/{customer_id}")
    assert updated_profile.status_code == 200
    updated_body = updated_profile.json()
    assert updated_body["verified"] is True
    assert updated_body.get("kyc_status", "verified") == "verified"

    outbound_resp = authenticated_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/transactions/outbound")
    assert outbound_resp.status_code == 200
